free_text: The reactions B-11(He3,n)N-13 and Li-7(He3,n)B-9 from
1.5 to 5.5 MeV bombarding energy.
5.5 MV Van de Graaff accelerator
A 300 V electrostatic secondary electron
suppressor was employed at the target to insure proper
beam integration.
Stilbene crystal 2.54 cm in diam. and 2.62 cm
long
It is believed that the relative cross sections are
correct to +-5% and any systematic error in the
magnitude of the absolute cross section is < +-30%.
Uncertainty in target thickness
Error in detector efficiency
Error in current integration
Error in detector geometry
Error in data reduction
Error in He-3 charge state
Errors in counting statistics 1 - 3%
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year
SD: Corrections in all Subents according to
new EXFOR rules. BIB updated. SAMPLE and part of
information from ERR-ANALYS moved to Subents 002-012.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
Targets of B-11 were made by
evaporating 96% enriched B-11 powder on a 0.05 cm
thick tungsten backing. Three B-11 targets having
thickness of 30, 50 and 160 ug/cm were used.
Fig 2

added. ERR-ANALYS, EN-SEC deleted. STATUS updated.
